resources.string: Suitefish is a lightweight, open-source app store client. It connects to an online catalog server, lists available apps and lets you download and install them.

You use Suitefish entirely at your own risk. This app installs software from an online source outside of official app stores.\n\nUsing a wrong, malicious or misconfigured custom server can expose you to vulnerabilities, malware or data leaks. Only connect to servers you fully trust. Neither the developer nor Suitefish is responsible for any damage, data loss or security incident resulting from the use of this app or of a custom server.

Suitefish is currently configured to use the default server.

When the default server is used, the app fetches its data (app lists, images and downloads) from the suitefish.com website. By continuing you accept the data policy published on suitefish.com. Please review that policy before using the app.

This notice is shown on every start.

Suitefish is currently configured to use a custom server:

%1$s

All app lists, images and downloads will be fetched from that server. Suitefish cannot verify the trustworthiness or privacy practices of custom servers — you are responsible for the server you connect to.

This notice is shown on every start.
Suitefish needs a catalog server to load apps from. You can use the default server or point the app at your own custom server. This choice is saved and can be changed later in Settings.
Connect to your own catalog server.
HTTPS is required and enforced. Enter a reachable online domain or IP. To use a port other than 443, append :port (e.g. example.com:8443).
I understand and accept the risks
Accept self-signed or otherwise untrusted HTTPS certificates. Only enable this for a server you control and trust.

Turning this on stops Suitefish from verifying the server\'s HTTPS certificate. Traffic can then be intercepted or modified by a man-in-the-middle, exposing you to malware or data leaks. Only enable this for a self-hosted server you fully trust.\n\nEnable anyway?
Invalid server. Enter a valid online domain or IP. HTTPS is enforced; only :port may be added.
Server could not be reached over HTTPS. Check the address and your connection.
No apps in the local catalog yet. Tap \"Sync catalog\" to fetch the list from the server.
None of your installed apps come from this Suitefish store, or the catalog has not been synced yet.
Server not reachable. Your last saved catalog was kept.

Suitefish installs apps that do not come from Google Play, so Android treats them as coming from an "unknown source".

When you install an app, Android may ask you to allow installations from Suitefish:

1. When prompted, tap Settings.
2. Enable "Allow from this source" (Install unknown apps) for Suitefish.
3. Go back and confirm the installation.

You can revoke this permission at any time under Android Settings > Apps > Special access > Install unknown apps. Only install apps you trust.
To check for updates, Suitefish will contact the online server for the installed Suitefish apps. Continue?
Suitefish will now contact the online server and download this app. Continue?
All Suitefish apps are up to date.
Suitefish could not reach the server, so this app could not be downloaded. Your device may be offline or the server may be down. Your saved catalog was kept.
Changing the server will DELETE all locally stored data (catalog, cached images and downloaded files) and restart the app fresh. Your installed apps are not removed. Continue?
Delete the local catalog, cached images and downloaded APKs. The app will restart.
A free, open-source app store client.

Suitefish is a community-friendly, open-source client for browsing and installing apps from a Suitefish catalog server. It is provided free of charge, with no ads and no tracking added by the app itself.
Made by Bugfish — \"Dreaming in Digital.\" Bugfish builds across Android, frameworks, JavaScript, games, Windows and web software.
Suitefish is open source. Source code, issues and contributions are welcome on GitHub.
Free & open source · Use at your own risk.
